summary: React Server Components (RSC) execute on the server by default in App Router. Client components opt in with `'use client'`. Crossing the boundary requires serializable props.
bestPractices: Mark client components with `'use client'` at the top of the file; never inside a function.
Pass server data into client subtrees through props, not via shared imports.
Keep client components leaf-shaped — Server Components compose them, not the other way around.
Use `Suspense` boundaries plus `loading.tsx` for streaming UX in long-running data paths.
antiPatterns: Importing `node:fs`, `node:crypto`, or other Node-only modules from a `'use client'` file.
Passing functions, class instances, or non-serializable values across the server→client boundary as props.
Re-marking the same module with `'use server'` and `'use client'` — these are mutually exclusive.
